'''Ian Edginton''' is a British comic book writer and the author of several ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comics]].

==Biography==
Edginton is best known for his steampunk/alternate history works and is the co-creator of [[Wikipedia:Scarlet Traces|''Scarlet Traces'']], a sequel to their adaptation of [[Wikipedia:H.G. Wells' The War of the Worlds (comic)|''H.G. Wells' The War of the Worlds'']]. With ''2000 AD'' we has written [[Wikipedia:Leviathan (comic)|''Leviathan'']], [[Wikipedia:Stickleback (comics)|''Stickleback'']] and, [[Wikipedia:The Red Seas|''The Red Seas'']] as well as one-off serials such as [[Wikipedia:American Gothic (comics)|''American Gothic'']] (2005).

His stories often have a torturous gestation. ''Scarlet Traces'' was an idea he had when first reading ''The War of the Worlds'', its first few installments appeared on Cool Beans website, before being serialized in the [[Wikipedia:Judge Dredd Megazine|''Judge Dredd Megazine'']]. Also ''The Red Seas'' was initially going to be drawn by Phil Winslade and be the final release by [[Wikipedia:Epic Comics|Epic]] but Winslade was still tied up with [[Wikipedia:Goddess (comic)|''Goddess'']] and when ideas for replacement artists were rejected Epic was finally wound up - the series only re-emerging when Edginton was pitching ideas to [[Wikipedia:Matt Smith (comics)|Matt Smith]] at the start of his ''2000 AD'' career.

With [[Wikipedia:D'Israeli|D'Israeli]] he has created a number of new series including ''Stickleback'', a tale of a strange villain in an alternate Victorian London, and ''Gothic'', which he describes as "Mary Shelley's Doc Savage". With Simon Davis he recently worked on a survival horror series, [[Wikipedia:Stone Island (2000AD)|''Stone Island'']], and he has also produced a comic version of the computer game [[Wikipedia:Hellgate: London|''Hellgate: London'']].

With Dan Abnett he is writing comics based on [[Wikipedia:Warhammer 40,000 comics|''Warhammer 40,000'']] [[Wikipedia:Damnation Crusade|''Damnation Crusade'']] and [[Wikipedia:Blood and Thunder (comics)|''Blood and Thunder'']])<ref>[http://www.comicbookresources.com/news/newsitem.cgi?id=8940 Edginton Lifts Off with "Warhammer 40,000"], Comic Book Resources, November 17, 2006</ref> and [[Wikipedia:Warhammer Fantasy (setting)#Fiction|''Warhammer'']] ([[Wikipedia:Forge of War|''Forge of War'']]) for Boom! Studios. The most recent is a Warhammer Fantasy series called [[Wikipedia:Condemned by Fire|''Condemned by Fire'']].<ref>[http://forum.newsarama.com/showthread.php?t=157564 Talking Warhammer with Ian Edginton], Newsarama, May 20, 2008</ref>

He is currently working on a dinosaurs and cowboys story called ''Sixgun Logic''. Also as part of Top Cow's Pilot Season he has written an [[Wikipedia:Angelus (comics)|''Angelus'']] one-shot.<ref name="angelus">[http://www.comicbookresources.com/news/newsitem.cgi?id=12462 Divine Retribution: Edginton talks ''Angelus: Pilot Season'' #1], Comic Book Resources, November 26, 2007</ref>

Following the [[Wikipedia:Wildstorm Universe#World's End|World's End]] event, Edginton will take over the writing of [[Wikipedia:Stormwatch: Post Human Division#Post-World's End|''Stormwatch: Post Human Division'']] from issue #13.<ref name="worldsend">[http://forum.newsarama.com/showthread.php?t=154293 NYCC '08: LIVING IN THE RUINS: WS Editor Ben Abernathy on 'Worlds End'], Newsarama, April 19, 2008</ref>

He is one of the few British comic talents to follow the reverse trajectory to the one usually taken: becoming successful in American comics before returning to work for [[Wikipedia:2000 AD (comic)|''2000 AD'']].
